clues.clue: This common mood disorder is marked by persistent sadness and loss of interest.
Excessive, hard-to-control worry and tension define this disorder, the 'generalized' form abbreviated GAD.
Swings between depressive lows and manic highs mark this disorder, once called manic depression.
After trauma, flashbacks and hypervigilance may signal this disorder abbreviated PTSD.
Compulsive, harmful drinking despite consequences is this 'use disorder.'
Most adults are advised to get at least this many hours of sleep nightly, the low end of the range.
This disorder causes repeated pauses in breathing during sleep; it's the 'sleep' ___.
Persistent difficulty falling or staying asleep is this condition.
The sleep stage of darting eyes and vivid dreaming is abbreviated this.
A bedside machine delivering pressurized air to keep airways open, abbreviated CPAP, treats this sleep disorder.
Persistent tiredness and low energy, common in depression, is this.
The inability to feel pleasure in normally enjoyable activities is this 'an-' term.
A racing heart, sweating and sudden dread peaking within minutes describe one of these 'attacks.'
Trouble concentrating and mental haziness from stress and poor sleep is nicknamed brain this.
Chronic low-grade depression lasting two years or more is this 'dys-' disorder.
This talk therapy, abbreviated CBT, reframes unhelpful thought patterns.
Fluoxetine and sertraline belong to this most-prescribed antidepressant class that raises serotonin.
This practice of calm, present-moment awareness is widely used to reduce stress.
This mood-stabilizing metal salt remains a classic treatment for bipolar disorder.
Many antidepressants work by raising levels of this 'feel-good' neurotransmitter.
This 'fight or flight' hormone surges during sudden acute stress.
Released by the adrenal glands during ongoing stress, this is often called the 'stress hormone.'
This almond-shaped brain region processes fear and threat.
This reward-and-motivation neurotransmitter is also tied to addictive behaviors.
This seahorse-shaped structure, vital to memory, can shrink under chronic stress.

final.clue: Aerobic exercise lifts mood partly by releasing these natural, pain-relieving 'feel-good' brain chemicals.
